name: Windows Event Log Cleared via Wevtutil (T1070.001)
description: Detects wevtutil.exe clearing event logs using the cl or clear-log subcommand. Ransomware and post-compromise tooling clears event logs to remove evidence of initial access and lateral movement before deploying payloads. Any log clearing that occurs outside an approved maintenance window — particularly when followed by encryption or shadow copy activity — should be escalated immediately.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "wevtutil.exe" and process.args : ("cl", "clear-log")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Windows Firewall Rule Manipulation via Netsh (T1562.004)
description: Detects netsh.exe adding, deleting, or modifying Windows Firewall rules via the advfirewall context. Threat actors manipulate firewall rules to open communication paths for C2 channels or to remove evidence of prior rule changes. Rapid add-then-delete sequences in particular indicate temporary port-forwarding or anti-forensic behaviour consistent with ransomware pre-staging.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "netsh.exe" and process.command_line : "*advfirewall*firewall*" and process.command_line : ("*add rule*", "*delete rule*", "*set rule*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: BitLocker Encryption Enabled via Manage-BDE with Password Protector (T1486)
description: Detects manage-bde.exe enabling BitLocker encryption with an explicit -Password protector on the command line. Legitimate enterprise BitLocker deployments rely on TPM or TPM+PIN protectors managed via Group Policy and are never initiated with a plaintext command-line password. The presence of -Password on a manage-bde -on command line is a high-confidence indicator of BitLocker-based ransomware: the attacker sets the password, encrypts the volume, and demands payment for decryption credentials.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "manage-bde.exe" and process.command_line : "*-on*" and process.command_line : "*-Password*"
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion - Ransomware Recovery Inhibition (T1490)
description: Detects deletion of Volume Shadow Copies via vssadmin.exe or wmic.exe. Ransomware operators systematically delete shadow copies immediately before or after encryption to prevent file restoration without paying the ransom. This is one of the strongest single-indicator signals for active ransomware. The /quiet flag confirms non-interactive, automated execution typical of malware. Correlate with manage-bde.exe activity on the same host for near-certain ransomware attribution.
query: process where event.type == "start" and ((process.name : "vssadmin.exe" and process.command_line : "*delete*shadows*") or (process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : "*shadowcopy*delete*") or (process.name : "wbadmin.exe" and process.command_line : "*delete*catalog*"))
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: BitLocker Ransomware Kill Chain - Encryption Followed by Shadow Copy Deletion (T1486, T1490)
description: High-confidence sequence detection: a BitLocker encryption command (manage-bde -on) followed within 5 minutes by a Volume Shadow Copy deletion command (vssadmin delete shadows or wmic shadowcopy delete) on the same host. This two-step sequence is the operational signature of BitLocker-based ransomware documented by NCC Group. The sequence has an extremely low false-positive rate in enterprise environments. An alert from this rule warrants immediate host isolation and incident response escalation.
query: sequence by host.id with maxspan=5m [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "manage-bde.exe" and process.command_line : "*-on*"] [process where event.type == "start" and ((process.name : "vssadmin.exe" and process.command_line : "*delete*shadows*") or (process.name : "wmic.exe" and process.command_line : "*shadowcopy*delete*"))]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
